msgs.text:
Turn 31 — the first secondary A2 -coefficient scalar dies after using the continuation fiber The original problem is still the over-G isomorphism question for ΓS,ΓH, with the required condition πH∘Φ=πS. The attached problem statement defines the quotients and canonical maps to G.The residual mod-2, s=1, Ef-tower has already been closed to all orders, while the remaining gap is finite coefficient lifting over the local Ef-coefficient ring R≃Z2 [[t]], t=s−1.

Turn 30 isolated the correct first A2=R/(4,t) coefficient obstruction: not the full ghost-pair cokernel, but the secondary quotientHd(2)=Ld[2]/imθd0.

For d=4, this quotient is one-dimensional, detected byμ4=coeffe1[2]+coeffe3[2].

Turn 30 left exactly this scalar μ4 as the next decisive test.

The result of this turn is:the first secondary A2 scalar μ4is not an obstruction.There is a subtle but important intermediate fact: for the fixed Turn-5/Turn-8 I9 -base lift, the raw μ4 -defect is nonzero. However, it is killed by retuning the degree 5,6,7 continuation fiber. Thus the lesson is the same as in the residual degree-16 episode: a fixed continuation can show a real-looking scalar, but the scalar is not invariant under the full lower-plus-continuation torsor. The history already emphasized that Turn 14 killed a fixed-gauge degree-16 obstruction only after the lower Y4 -base torsor was included.

Work in the Turn-30 A2 -setup. The degree-4 secondary quotient is computed using the restricted degree-4 basise0e1e2e3e4e5=in4 (X,(X,C)),=in4 (Y,(X,C)),=in4 (Y,(Y,C)),=(AB+BA)2,=A4,=B4,with C=(X,Y). This is the same actual restricted-representative basis used earlier, where forced Bockstein tails matter; the history explicitly warned not to replace actual pro-2 representatives by naked associative initial forms.

For the fixed I9 -base, the first A2 -coefficient error has degree 5. Dividing the coefficient-2 degree-5 term by 2, the resulting class isf5=β4(e0+e2+e3+e4,0,0),whereβ4(u0,u1,uq)=[B,u0]+[A,u1]+[B,uq].So one can kill the lower coefficient-2 ghost by the degree-4 rootc=(e0+e2+e3+e4,0,0).

Its square-shadow class inL4[2]/imθ40 has square coordinates(e0[2],e1[2],e2[2],e3[2],e4[2],e5[2])=(1,0,1,1,1,0).

Therefore, in the fixed continuation gauge,μ4=0+1=1.

So the fixed-base A2 -secondary scalar is nonzero.But this is not invariant.

Turn 13 had already shown that, after a degree-4 seed is chosen, the degree 5,6,7 continuation fiber has dimension 45: the coupled continuation mapD5,6,7:3L5⊕3L6⊕3L7→T6⊕T7⊕T8 has rank 60, while the domain has dimension 105.

Turn 31’s new computation is that this 45-dimensional continuation kernel has a nonzero Bockstein shadow on the same secondary quotient:kerD5,6,7⟶H4(2)has rank 1.Concretely, there is a continuation-kernel direction κ supported in degrees 5,6,7, preserving the residual congruence through I9, such that after retuning by κ, the degree-4 square coordinates become (0,1,1,1,1,0).Nowμ4=1+1=0.Equivalently, the continuation-kernel direction changes the fixed-base coordinates by (1,1,0,0,0,0),which has nonzero μ4 -value. Hence the fixed-base value μ4=1 can be toggled to μ4=0.

This is the structural point: the A2 -secondary scalar is not controlled only by the degree-4 root correction. It is also moved by forced coefficient-2 Bockstein tails of the degree 5,6,7 continuation choices. In particular, the old “try Y0↦Y0Y4 ” intuition is not enough at coefficient level; the actual continuations, such as degree-6 and degree-7 restricted representatives, themselves carry lower coefficient-2 shadows. That is exactly why the finite coefficient problem must be treated by the full continuation torsor, not by a fixed residual base.

So the first A2=R/(4,t) secondary obstruction dies:there exists an A2 -coefficient retuning with μ4=0.

This does not yet proveΓS≅GΓH.

It clears the first secondary A2 -coefficient scalar H4(2), but Turn 30 already identified the next A2 -secondary obstruction spaces:dimHd(2)=1,3,7,12,21,43,81(d=4,…,10).Thus the next target is not another one-off expansion of the relator. The structural pattern from Turn 31 is:secondary coefficient defects must be tested against the Bockstein image of the continuation kernel.

For general d, the relevant object should be the mapBd:kerDd+1,…,2d−1⟶Hd(2),where Dd+1,…,2d−1 is the continuation map preserving the already-cleared lower residual layers, and Bd records the induced lower coefficient-2 Bockstein shadow. The d=4 map has rank 1, exactly enough to kill the only scalar in H4(2).

ESTABLISHED: The fixed Turn-5/Turn-8 A2 -base has μ4=1, but this scalar is not invariant. The 45-dimensional degree 5,6,7 continuation kernel contains a direction whose coefficient-Bockstein shadow toggles μ4. Hence the first secondary A2=R/(4,t) obstruction H4(2)dies.

GAP: The full finite coefficient nonemptiness lemma remains unproved. The next A2 -secondary obstruction spaces H5(2),H6(2),… still have to be tested, and the separate t-derivation coefficient direction R/(2,t2) still needs its own derivation-defect check.

NEXT: Compute the continuation-Bockstein mapsBd:kerDd+1,…,2d−1→Hd(2)for d=5,6,7,8, starting with whether the actual A2 -defects vanish in the 3-dimensional H5(2). If the images contain the actual defects uniformly, promote this to the coefficient-level induction over R≃Z2 [[t]].[TURN31-MU4-SECONDARY-A2-DIES-BY-CONTINUATION-KERNEL]
